# 🩸 Heresy Rising — Pitch Document

**Version:** 1.0.0
**Status:** 🔒 Locked
**Last updated:** 2026-07-05 — design-lock pass: two-faction map, interrogation-pivot, cripple-not-kill, drift gated by heretical catalyst
**Source:** Heretic + Nicolas, 2026-07-05 design-lock session
**Companion files:** `mechanics/day-phase.md`, `mechanics/heretic-kit.md`, `mechanics/loyalist-kit.md`, `mechanics/drift.md`, `mechanics/interrogation.md`, `mechanics/setup.md`, `OPEN_QUESTIONS.md`

---

## Changelog

### 1.0.0 (2026-07-05)
- Two-faction map (v1): Loyalist / Heretic. Puritan / Radical subfactions deferred to Phase 2.
- Interrogation pivot canonical — replaces lynching as primary day-action
- Cripple-not-kill pillar locked — stat damage preserves player count
- Drift gated by heretical catalyst — no auto-flip on max drift
- Drifting player gets private system cues only; no numeric drift display ever

---

## 🩸 TL;DR (one-liner)

> **Heresy Rising** is a 40k-flavored social deduction game where nobody dies — they get *interrogated*. The Inquisition cripples suspects instead of killing them. And every powerful action warps the wielder.

---

## 📜 Short pitch (Discord / chat length, ~80 words)

> I'm designing a Werewolf-style social deduction game set in Warhammer 40,000 — **Heresy Rising**. Two twists: **(1)** lynching is replaced by **interrogation** — suspects get *crippled* (stat-damage), not killed. Death is rare. The table breaks each other down over rounds, not bullets. **(2)** Every player has a hidden **drift** meter. Every vote, every night action, every murder witnessed… it all adds to the rot. But drift never resets — and the only way to *flip* to Heretic is a heretical ritual performed on you. Power = drift. The slow grind is the game.

---

## 🎯 Medium pitch (one paragraph, ~150 words)

> **Heresy Rising** is a social deduction game in the Warhammer 40,000 universe, built on Werewolf's bones but rebuilt around two ideas: **interrogation instead of lynching**, and **corruption you can feel but not see**.
>
> Players form an Inquisitorial cell: loyal Citizens, Interrogators, Medicae, Novice-Psykers, Arbitrators — and hidden Heretics among them. Each day, the table debates and votes: *interrogate this round* (cripple the suspect, no death) or *lynch this round* (cripple-to-max, kill, alignment revealed). Most days are interrogations. Lynch is the escalation lever.
>
> **Drift** is a hidden corruption meter. It rises from actions — wrong lynch, witnessing violence, using high-power abilities, voting with the losing side. Drift never resets. The only way to actually *flip* a Loyalist to Heretic is for a heretical catalyst (a ritual, a touch, a pact) to be performed on them when they're already maxed. Until then, drift is just *capability loss*. The heretic is in you. The ritual makes it real.
>
> The differentiator: in every other Werewolf, death removes players. Here, **crippling** removes *capability*. The game ends when one side has parity — or when the table is broken.

---

## 📖 Long pitch (full page, one breath)

> In the grim darkness of the far future, the Inquisition does not execute suspects — it *interrogates* them. A heretic caught in the field is too valuable to waste. Their mind holds secrets. Their soul holds warp-taint. The Interrogator's chair does the rest.
>
> **Heresy Rising** is a social deduction game in the Warhammer 40,000 universe. Players form an Inquisitorial cell: loyal Citizens, Interrogators, Chirurgeons, Novice-Psykers, Arbitrators — and Heretics hiding among them.
>
> Each **day**, the table debates. They vote on the *mode*: **interrogate** (cripple a suspect, stat damage, no death) or **lynch** (cripple-to-max, kill, alignment revealed publicly). Interrogation is the default. Lynch is the lever you pull when interrogation has failed. Most days, someone walks away crippled. Players rarely die.
>
> Each **night**, the powers act. The Heretics' chosen target. The Chirurgeon's protected soul. The Novice-Psychic's drift-hint about a player. The Interrogator's cumulative pressure on a suspect.
>
> And under all of it, **drift** works. Every wrong vote, every witnessed murder, every high-power ability, every loss — drift rises. The meter is **hidden**. Players get *hints* — *"you feel a chill,"* *"the Emperor's light feels further today"* — but never a number. Drift never resets. If you max out, you stay maxed. You just keep *functioning* at lower capacity. Until a heretical catalyst is performed on you — and then you flip.
>
> That's the lever that breaks loyalty: not the meter itself, but the *ritual* that converts a maxed player. Heretics can't mass-convert. They can only burn the already-burned.
>
> The **Imperial Citizen** is the default — pure voter, no night power, voice and survival. The **Interrogator** is the political investigator — applies cumulative pressure on a suspect, day after day, until the story cracks. The **Novice-Psychic** is barely-trained, but *feels* warp-stink on people — qualitative drift hints. The **Chirurgeon** keeps the cell alive — protects one player per night. The **Arbitrator** is the bodyguard — takes the hit for someone else.
>
> On the Heretic side, a hidden cult that hunts at night. They have a *heretical catalyst* — the ritual that flips max-drift players. Without it, they're just killers. With it, they're corrupting souls.
>
> The game ends when the Heretics reach parity (their living count ≥ Loyalists) or when the cell breaks.
>
> **Why it's different:** There are a thousand Werewolves. None of them make death feel *rare*. None of them replace lynching with a stat-damage interrogation mechanic. None of them have a corruption meter that *only matters when paired with a ritual*. This isn't Werewolf with 40k paint — it's a different game that uses Werewolf's skeleton.

---

## 🎨 Taglines & one-liners (grab any)

- *Nobody dies. They get interrogated.*
- *Crippling is the new killing.*
- *Drift is the rot. The ritual is the door.*
- *You can lie about your drift. You can't lie about the chair.*
- *Power = drift. Drift ≠ conversion. Conversion needs a ritual.*
- *The heretic isn't born. The heretic is burned. Slowly. Then flipped.*
- *There are a thousand Werewolves. None of them make the chair feel this real.*

---

## 🔑 The hook (one sentence)

> **In every other Werewolf, you kill the heretic. In Heresy Rising, you sit them in a chair and break them down — and meanwhile, you're drifting too.**

---

## 📍 Current design pillars (for context when pitching)

| # | Pillar | Description |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Werewolf structure | Day / night, hidden roles, voting |
| 2 | Hidden drift meter | Every player, every game, rises through actions |
| 3 | Drift caused by actions | Wrong lynch, witnessing violence, high-power actions, losing-side vote |
| 4 | Drift never resets | Only locks in at max. Convertible Drift is the one exception. |
| 5 | Interrogation replaces lynching | Day-phase action: cripple (no death) by default |
| 6 | Lynch = escalation lever | Cripple-to-max, then death, alignment revealed |
| 7 | Cripple-not-kill | Player count stays stable. Capability loss is the lever. |
| 8 | Drift visibility = faint hints | System cues, never a public stat |
| 9 | Drift gated by heretical catalyst | Max drift alone doesn't flip. A ritual on a maxed player does. |
| 10 | Two factions | Loyalist / Heretic. Puritan and Radical subfactions come later. |
| 11 | Heretic win = parity | Heretics win when their living count ≥ Loyalists |
| 12 | Power = risk | Stronger roles drift faster |
| 13 | Chat-based | Played in Telegram / Discord, not around a table |
| 14 | Drift MAX = 20 | Numerical lock 2026-07-06 (was 10, bumped after Haru feedback) |
| 15 | Drift zones (Green/Yellow/Orange/Red/Black) | Numerical lock 2026-07-06 — intel noise scales with zone |
| 16 | Nightly sleep recovery = −1 (skips action) | Numerical lock 2026-07-06 |
| 17 | Priest stack (Loyalist + Heretic) | Numerical lock 2026-07-06 — three sermon tiers each |

---

## ❓ Known open questions (be honest about when pitching)

- Interrogation vote threshold (majority? plurality? supermajority?) — *ledger: open*
- Drift trigger magnitudes per tier — **LOCKED 2026-07-06** (T0=-1, T1=+1, T2=+2, T3=+3; wrong lynch=+2; voted with losing side=+1, halts day 5+)
- High-power action tiering — **LOCKED 2026-07-06** (T1/T2/T3 mapping above)
- Drift MAX value — **LOCKED 2026-07-06 at 20** (was 10)
- Threshold zones & intel noise — **LOCKED 2026-07-06** (Green/Yellow/Orange/Red/Black, noise rates 0/0.20/0.40/0.80/1.0)
- Priest + Heretic Priest — **LOCKED 2026-07-06 numerically** (Whisper/Hymn/Litany; False Comfort/Twisted Hymn/Warp Litany)
- Drift pollutes intel — **LOCKED 2026-07-06 at zone crossing**
- Sleep-forfeits-on-action — **LOCKED 2026-07-06** (sleep = -1, requires skipping action)
- Heretic drift cap = 13 by day 3 — **LOCKED 2026-07-06**

### Still pending design-clarity

- Anti-Priest mechanic: ship OR cut (conflict with heretical catalyst gate)
- Cults: pool resources / sync actions (Phase 2)
- 5 Heretic roles (currently penciled: Murderer, Heretic Priest, Conspirator, Saboteur, Recruiter — *not locked*)
- Puritan/Radical subfaction layer (Phase 2, deferred)
- Does successful action refund drift as relief sink? (Haru floated; not locked)
- Long-game behavior past day 5 (wrong-lynch also halt? currently no)

---

## 📤 Outbound formats

The pitches above are written for general use. Here are the formats Nicolas actually needs day-to-day.

### Discord-ready Markdown (copy-paste friendly)

**Short — for one Discord message:**

```markdown
**Heresy Rising** — a 40k social deduction game where nobody dies, they get *interrogated*.

You know those werewolf games where you lynch someone? In this one, the table votes each day: *interrogate* (cripple, no death) or *lynch* (the escalation lever). Most days, someone walks away broken. The table grinds each other down over rounds.

Every player has a hidden **drift** meter. Wrong votes, witnessed violence, high-power actions — it all adds up. But drift *never resets*, and the only way to flip a Loyalist to Heretic is a heretical ritual performed on them when they're maxed. The rot is in everyone. The ritual is the door.

*Power = drift.* There are a thousand Werewolves; this one breaks you down instead of killing you.
```

**One-liner — for a single chat bubble:**

```markdown
I'm designing a 40k Werewolf variant where lynching is replaced by *interrogation* — suspects get crippled, not killed — and every player has a hidden drift meter that only converts via a heretical ritual. **Heresy Rising**.
```

**Three-bullet pitch — for a quick share:**

```markdown
**Heresy Rising** — 40k social deduction, Werewolf bones rebuilt:

• Day action is **interrogate** (cripple, no death) by default; **lynch** is the rare escalation.
• Every player has hidden **drift** — never resets, but only flips via heretical ritual.
• Roles: Imperial Citizen, Interrogator, Chirurgeon, Novice-Psychic, Arbitrator (+ 5 Heretic roles).
• Heretics win by **parity**, not by body count.
```

**Friend-pitch — medium length, story-forward:**

```markdown
The pitch I usually give:

You know Werewolf — hidden roles, day voting, night kills? This is that, but the Inquisition is doing the hunting, and they don't kill suspects. They *interrogate* them. You sit in a chair for a while, you walk out crippled. Some days the table votes to lynch instead — but that's the lever, not the default.

The twist nobody else has: **drift**. Every player has a hidden corruption meter. Every vote, every night action, every murder you witness — it adds up. But it never resets. And critically: drift alone doesn't flip you. You can max out and stay Loyalist your whole game if no one touches you. The only way to actually *become* a Heretic is for a Heretic to perform a ritual on you when you're already maxed.

So the Heretics can't mass-convert. They can only burn the already-burned. The Loyalists have time to find them. But every powerful action the Loyalists take to fight back… *also* raises their own drift.

It's Werewolf with a corruption meter that only matters when paired with a ritual, and a torture chair instead of a gallows. Called **Heresy Rising**.
```
